title: Chevrolet Impala debuts
subtitle: Car model by General Motors
fact: The Chevrolet Impala is a full-size car that was built by Chevrolet for model years 1958 to 1985, 1994 to 1996, and 2000 to 2020.

title: Ford Model T debuts
subtitle: Car model by Ford Motor Company
fact: The Ford Model T is an automobile that was produced by the Ford Motor Company from October 1, 1908, to May 26, 1927.

title: Dodge Charger debuts
subtitle: Car model by Stellantis North America
fact: The Dodge Charger is a model of automobile marketed by Dodge in various forms over eight generations since 1966.

title: Mini debuts
subtitle: Car model by British Motor Corporation
fact: The Mini is a two-door, four-seat small car produced for four decades over a single generation, with many names and variants, by the British Motor Corporation (BMC) and its successors British Leyland and the Rover Group, and finally (briefly) under BMW ownership.

title: Bugatti Chiron debuts
subtitle: Car model by
fact: The Bugatti Chiron is a mid-engine two-seater sports car designed and developed in Germany by Bugatti Engineering GmbH.

title: Audi A8 debuts
subtitle: Car model by Audi AG
fact: The Audi A8 is a full-size luxury sedan manufactured and marketed by the German automaker Audi since 1994.

title: Mini Hatch debuts
subtitle: Car model by
fact: The Mini is a supermini car which has been made since April 2001.

title: Škoda Octavia debuts
subtitle: Car model by Škoda Auto
fact: The Škoda Octavia is a small family car (C-segment) produced by the Czech car manufacturer Škoda Auto since the end of 1996.

title: Chevrolet Caprice debuts
subtitle: Car model by General Motors
fact: The Chevrolet Caprice is a full-size car produced by Chevrolet in North America from 1965 through the 1996 model years.

title: Chevrolet Monte Carlo debuts
subtitle: Car model by General Motors
fact: The Chevrolet Monte Carlo is a two-door coupe that was manufactured and marketed by the Chevrolet division of General Motors.

title: Ford Escape debuts
subtitle: Car model by Ford Motor Company
fact: The Ford Escape is a compact crossover SUV manufactured and marketed by the Ford Motor Company from the 2001 to the 2026 model years across four generations, as Ford's first SUV derived from a car platform.

title: Ford Pinto debuts
subtitle: Car model by Ford Motor Company
fact: The Ford Pinto is a subcompact car that was manufactured and marketed by Ford Motor Company in North America from 1970 until 1980.

title: Chevrolet El Camino debuts
subtitle: Car model by General Motors
fact: The Chevrolet El Camino is a coupé utility vehicle that was produced by Chevrolet between 1959 and 1960, and 1964 and 1987.

title: Mercedes-Benz W126 debuts
subtitle: Car model by Daimler-Benz AG
fact: The Mercedes-Benz W126 is a series of passenger cars made by Daimler-Benz AG.

title: Ford Fusion Americas debuts
subtitle: Car model by Ford Motor Company
fact: The Ford Fusion is a mid-size car that was manufactured and marketed by the Ford Motor Company.

title: Lamborghini Gallardo debuts
subtitle: Car model by Lamborghini
fact: The Lamborghini Gallardo is a sports car built by the Italian automotive manufacturer Lamborghini from 2003 to 2013.

title: Ford Thunderbird debuts
subtitle: Car model by Ford Motor Company
fact: The Ford Thunderbird is a personal luxury car manufactured and marketed by Ford Motor Company for model years 1955 to 2005, with a hiatus from 1998 to 2001.

title: Benz Patent-Motorwagen debuts
subtitle: Car model by Carl Benz
fact: The Benz Patent-Motorwagen, built in 1885 by the German engineer Karl Benz, is widely regarded as the first practical automobile and was the first car put into production.

title: Mercedes-Benz W210 debuts
subtitle: Car model by Mercedes-Benz Group
fact: The Mercedes-Benz W210 is the internal designation for a range of executive cars manufactured by Mercedes-Benz and marketed under the E-Class model name in both sedan/saloon (1995–2002) and station wagon/estate (1996–2003) configurations.

title: Ford Taurus debuts
subtitle: Car model by Ford Motor Company
fact: The Ford Taurus is an automobile that was manufactured and marketed by the Ford Motor Company in the United States from 1985 to 2019.

title: Cadillac de Ville series debuts
subtitle: Car model by Cadillac
fact: The Cadillac DeVille is a model name used by Cadillac over eight generations, originally to designate a trim level of the 1949 Cadillac Series 62 and later for a standalone model in the brand range.

title: Ford Mondeo debuts
subtitle: Car model by Ford of Europe
fact: The Ford Mondeo is a mid-size/large family car (D-segment) that was manufactured and marketed by Ford across five generations of models from 1993 to 2022.

title: Plymouth Barracuda debuts
subtitle: Car model by Plymouth
fact: The Plymouth Barracuda is a two-door pony car that was manufactured by Chrysler Corporation from 1964 through 1974 model years.

title: Tesla Model X debuts
subtitle: Car model by Tesla
fact: The Tesla Model X is a battery electric mid-size luxury crossover SUV built by Tesla, Inc. since 2015.

title: Ford Capri debuts
subtitle: Car model by Ford of Europe
fact: The Ford Capri is a car which was built by Ford of Europe from 1968 to 1986.

title: Ford Torino debuts
subtitle: Car model by Ford Motor Company
fact: The Ford Torino is an automobile that was produced by Ford for the North American market between 1968 and 1976.

title: Chevrolet Corvair debuts
subtitle: Car model by General Motors
fact: The Chevrolet Corvair is a rear-engined, air-cooled compact car manufactured and marketed by Chevrolet over two generations from the 1960 through 1969 model years.

title: Ford Galaxie debuts
subtitle: Car model by Ford Motor Company
fact: The Ford Galaxie is a full-size automobile that was sold by the Ford Motor Company in North America from the 1959 to 1974 model years.

title: Buick Riviera debuts
subtitle: Car model by General Motors
fact: The Buick Riviera is a personal luxury car that was marketed by Buick from 1963 to 1999, with the exception of the 1994 model year.

title: Mercury Grand Marquis debuts
subtitle: Car model by Ford Motor Company
fact: The Mercury Grand Marquis is an automobile that was produced by Mercury from the 1975 until 2011 model years.

title: Kia morning debuts
subtitle: Car model by Kia
fact: The Kia Picanto is a city car that has been produced by the South Korean car manufacturer, Kia, since 2003.

title: Ferrari Daytona debuts
subtitle: Car model by Ferrari
fact: The Ferrari Daytona is a two-seat grand tourer produced by Ferrari from 1968 to 1973.

title: Dacia Logan debuts
subtitle: Car model by Dacia
fact: The Dacia Logan is a family of automobiles produced and marketed jointly by the French manufacturer Renault and its Romanian subsidiary Dacia since mid-2004, and was the successor to the Dacia 1310 and Dacia Solenza.

title: Volkswagen Arteon debuts
subtitle: Car model by Volkswagen
fact: The Volkswagen Arteon is a car manufactured by German car manufacturer Volkswagen.

title: Ford Sierra debuts
subtitle: Car model by Ford of Europe
fact: The Ford Sierra is a mid-size/large family car manufactured and marketed by Ford of Europe from 1982–1993.

title: BMW M1 debuts
subtitle: Car model by
fact: The BMW M1 is a mid-engined sports car produced by German automotive manufacturer BMW from 1978 until 1981.

title: Ford EcoSport debuts
subtitle: Car model by Ford do Brasil
fact: The Ford EcoSport is a subcompact crossover SUV (B-segment) manufactured by Ford between 2003 and 2022.

title: Ferrari Roma debuts
subtitle: Car model by Ferrari
fact: The Ferrari Roma is a grand touring car by Italian manufacturer Ferrari.

title: Nissan Bluebird debuts
subtitle: Car model by Nissan
fact: The Nissan Bluebird is a compact car produced between 1955 and 2007 with a model name introduced in 1957.

title: Ford Anglia debuts
subtitle: Car model by Ford Motor Company
fact: The Ford Anglia is a small family car that was designed and manufactured by Ford UK.

title: Ford Ka debuts
subtitle: Car model by Pininfarina
fact: The Ford Ka is a small car which was manufactured by Ford Motor Company from 1996 to 2021 over three generations.

title: Ferrari Purosangue debuts
subtitle: Car model by Ferrari
fact: The Ferrari Purosangue is a high-performance mid-size luxury crossover SUV by Italian automobile manufacturer Ferrari that was introduced on 13 September 2022.

title: Polestar 2 debuts
subtitle: Car model by Polestar
fact: The Polestar 2 is a battery electric 5-door liftback marketed by Polestar, an affiliate of Geely Holding and Volvo Cars.

title: Chrysler Neon debuts
subtitle: Car model by Fiat
fact: The Neon is a compact car built from November 1993 until 2005 by the American Chrysler Corporation over two generations.

title: Jaguar XJ220 debuts
subtitle: Car model by Jaguar Cars
fact: The Jaguar XJ220 is a two-seat supercar produced by British luxury car manufacturer Jaguar from 1992 until 1994, in collaboration with the specialist automotive and race engineering company Tom Walkinshaw Racing.

title: Volkswagen ID.3 debuts
subtitle: Car model by Volkswagen Group
fact: The Volkswagen ID.3 is a battery electric small family car (C-segment) produced by Volkswagen since 2019.

title: Ford Granada Europe debuts
subtitle: Car model by Ford of Britain
fact: The European Ford Granada is an executive car manufactured by Ford Europe from 1972 until 1994.

title: Ford Zephyr debuts
subtitle: Car model by Ford of Britain
fact: The Ford Zephyr is an executive car manufactured by Ford of Britain from 1950 until 1972.

title: Bugatti EB110 debuts
subtitle: Car model by Bugatti Automobili
fact: The Bugatti EB 110 is a mid-engine sports car initially conceived by Paolo Stanzani in the mid 1980s and produced by Bugatti Automobili S.p.A. from 1991 until 1995, when the company was liquidated.

title: Mercedes-Benz W111 debuts
subtitle: Car model by Daimler-Benz AG
fact: The Mercedes-Benz W111 is a line of full-sized luxury cars produced by Mercedes-Benz between 1959 and 1971, including four-door saloons (1959–1968) and two-door coupés and cabriolets (1961–1971).

title: Citroën SM debuts
subtitle: Car model by Peugeot
fact: The Citroën SM is a high-performance coupé produced by the French manufacturer Citroën from 1970 to 1975.

title: Ford Orion debuts
subtitle: Car model by Ford of Europe
fact: The Ford Orion is a small family car that was produced by Ford Europe from 1983 until 1993.

title: Ford Taunus debuts
subtitle: Car model by Ford Germany
fact: The Ford Taunus is a family car manufactured and marketed by Ford Germany throughout Europe.

title: Mercedes-Benz EQS debuts
subtitle: Car model by Mercedes-Benz Group
fact: The Mercedes-Benz EQS (V297) is a battery electric full-size luxury liftback car produced by German automobile manufacturer Mercedes-Benz Group.

title: Ford Laser debuts
subtitle: Car model by Mazda
fact: The Ford Laser is a compact car, originally a subcompact car in the first three generations, which was sold by Ford in Asia, Oceania, and parts of South America and Africa.

title: Škoda Enyaq iV debuts
subtitle: Car model by Škoda Auto
fact: The Škoda Enyaq is a battery electric compact crossover SUV manufactured by Škoda Auto.

title: Ford Scorpio debuts
subtitle: Car model by Ford Germany
fact: The Ford Scorpio is an executive car that was produced by Ford Germany from 1985 to 1998.

title: Rolls-Royce Phantom VII debuts
subtitle: Car model by
fact: The Rolls-Royce Phantom is a full-sized luxury saloon car made by Rolls-Royce Motor Cars.

title: Mercedes-Benz Citan debuts
subtitle: Car model by Mercedes-Benz Group
fact: The Mercedes-Benz Citan is a panel van and leisure activity vehicle introduced as a badge-engineered variant of the Renault Kangoo in 2012 and marketed by Mercedes-Benz as the successor to the Vaneo compact MPV.

title: Ford Fusion Europe debuts
subtitle: Car model by Ford Germany
fact: The European Ford Fusion is a car manufactured and marketed by Ford Europe from 2002 to 2012 across a single generation.

title: Lamborghini Estoque debuts
subtitle: Car model by Lamborghini
fact: The Lamborghini Estoque is a one-off concept car built by Italian automobile manufacturer Lamborghini.

title: Citroën C4 Cactus debuts
subtitle: Car model by Citroën
fact: The Citroën C4 Cactus is a subcompact crossover SUV, produced by French automaker Citroën in Spain between April 2014 and December 2017, with production of the second generation commencing in October 2017, with the final months of production being disrupted by the COVID-19 pandemic.

title: Opel Kapitän debuts
subtitle: Car model by Opel
fact: The Opel Kapitän is a luxury car made in several different generations by the German car manufacturer Opel from 1938 until 1970.

title: Goggomobil debuts
subtitle: Car model by Glas
fact: Goggomobil was a series of microcars produced by Hans Glas in the Bavarian town of Dingolfing between 1955 and 1969.

title: Daewoo Matiz debuts
subtitle: Car model by General Motors
fact: The Chevrolet Spark is a city car manufactured by General Motors's subsidiary GM Korea from 1998 to 2022.
